best.code:
access_type = row['type']

# Non-LOAD types are almost never evicted (decision=0 only for LOAD)
if access_type != 0:
    return 1

# From here: access_type == 0 (LOAD)
# LOAD has ~20% eviction rate; need to separate classes with minimal constants

ip = row['ip']
addr = row['full_addr']

# Page-level alignment (strongest predictor)
ip_page = ip >> 12
addr_page = addr >> 12

# Same page = strong cache-friendly signal
if ip_page == addr_page:
    return 2

# Different pages: use page distance and bit-level correlation
page_dist = abs(ip_page - addr_page)

# Cache line offset (low 6 bits)
ip_offset = ip & 63
addr_offset = addr & 63

# Compute XOR for bit-level proximity (Hamming distance as tiebreaker)
xor_val = ip ^ addr
bit_dist = bin(xor_val).count('1')

# Combine signals: nearby pages + low bit distance = reuse likely
score = 0

if page_dist < 16:
    score += 2
elif page_dist < 256:
    score += 1

if bit_dist < 20:
    score += 1
elif bit_dist > 48:
    score -= 2

# Offset correlation as refinement
if ip_offset == addr_offset:
    score += 1
elif abs(ip_offset - addr_offset) > 32:
    score -= 1

return score
